item.title: Birds of War | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: Colombia is home to about 2,000 bird species, more than anywhere else on Earth. Decades of conflict kept people out of many areas, preserving the birds' habitat. Now birders are flocking in. Anderson Cooper reports, Sunday.

item.title: Trump administration to cut 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany
item.description: President Trump said earlier this week he was reviewing the possible reduction of troops in Germany, which hosts more than 35,000 U.S. service members.

item.title: Inspiring and funny moments from Artemis II astronauts' town hall
item.description: Artemis II astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen shared details about their historic trip around the moon, including funny moments, during a CBS News town hall. The astronauts were also joined by "Apollo 13" director Ron Howard. Here are a few highlights.

item.title: 2 employees killed in Kentucky bank robbery, person of interest in custody
item.description: Kentucky State Police said a man went to a U.S. Bank in Berea, Kentucky, and shot and killed a man and a woman, both employees at the bank.

item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for May 3, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Kevin Hassett, director of the White House National Economic Council; Democratic Sen. Rev. Raphael Warnock of Georgia; Democratic Rep. Jason Crow of Colorado; Neel Kashkari, president and CEO of the Minneapolis Fed, and Mike Wirth, Chevron CEO and chairman.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein may have left a suicide note, NYT reports
item.description: The late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein may have left a suicide note that has been kept sealed for years, The New York Times reports. According to the NYT, Epstein's cellmate discovered the note after Epstein was first reported to have attempted suicide. CBS News crime and public safety senior coordinating producer Anna Schecter breaks down what to know.

item.title: What happens if Spirit Airlines shuts down?
item.description: Spirit Airlines may cease operations as soon as Saturday after negotiations over a government aid package stalled. CBS News travel editor Peter Greenberg explains what it looks like when an air carrier shuts down.

item.title: Body found in Tampa Bay identified as 2nd missing doctoral student
item.description: Officials identified human remains that kayakers found in Tampa Bay as those of a second missing University of South Florida student. CBS News correspondent Cristian Benavides reports.

item.title: Trump "not satisfied" with new peace deal offered by Iran
item.description: Iran said it had offered a new proposal to the U.S. to end the war, as the Strait of Hormuz standoff sends costs soaring around the world.

item.title: Ford CEO Jim Farley says most new models will be "more affordable versions"
item.description: Ford announced a major affordability push on Friday, offering employee pricing on nearly all its new vehicles through the Fourth of July. CBS News senior transportation correspondent Kris Van Cleave spoke exclusively with Ford CEO Jim Farley.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from ending deportation protections for Yemenis
item.description: A federal judge on Friday blocked the Trump administration from revoking legal protections for more than 2,800 Yemeni nationals.

item.title: Disaster Tourists | Sunday on 60 Minutes
item.description: Often following natural disasters, conspiracists, militias, and white supremacist groups sweep in to hard-hit communities offering help. But they've been called "disaster tourists," out to soften their image, gain followers and sow doubt in the government. Sunday.

item.title: FDA expands access to drug helping pancreatic cancer patients
item.description: Former Sen. Ben Sasse, 54, called daraxonrasib "a miracle drug" that was allowing him to live longer and with less pain.

item.title: Final defendant sentenced in Zaria McKeever murder case
item.description: Parker, who admitted lying to investigators and sheltering her son after he sent gunmen to kill his ex-girlfriend, is the last of five people sentenced in the November 2022 Brooklyn Park murder.

item.title: Trump tells Congress "hostilities" with Iran have "terminated"
item.description: President Trump on Friday told Congress that hostilities with Iran have "terminated," addressing a critical 60-day deadline.

item.title: Comey faces charges over "86 47" post. How strong is the case?
item.description: Former FBI Director James Comey was charged with two counts arising out a now-deleted image he shared on Instagram that showed seashells arranged to read "86 47."

item.title: Body found in Tampa Bay identified as 2nd missing doctoral student
item.description: Nahida Bristy and Zamil Limon were last seen​ in the Tampa area on April 16. Limon's roommate has been charged with their murders.

item.title: As Iran war hits key 60-day deadline, Congress and Trump face choices
item.description: The War Powers Resolution sets deadlines for the president to end hostilities without congressional approval.

item.title: Spirit Airlines could shut down as soon as Saturday
item.description: Negotiations over a $500 million dollar government aid package for Spirit stalled after bondholders balked at the terms.

item.title: Gold House reveals new Gold100 list
item.description: Gold House revealed its 2026 Gold100 list honoring Asian Pacific culture. Co-founder and CEO Bing Chen joins CBS News with more.

item.title: Lizzo explains what the public gets wrong about her: "I'm just shining a light"
item.description: Lizzo sits down with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King to discuss what the public misunderstands about her.

item.title: Bill Nye on meeting the Artemis II crew and his take on the historic moon mission
item.description: Bill Nye The Science Guy, the chief ambassador of The Planetary Society, joins CBS News 24/7 Mornings with more after meeting the Artemis II crew in person after their successful mission around the moon.

item.title: Meet the horses running in the Kentucky Derby
item.description: The Kentucky Derby will see a full field of 20 horses in the first leg of the 2026 competition for horse racing's Triple Crown.

item.title: Artemis crew surprises 5-year-old aspiring astronaut with special gift
item.description: The Artemis II team gained a new member, and the crew made sure their youngest teammate had the right stuff for space.

item.title: Artemis crew tells kids moon flight was "the best roller coaster ride"
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts joined "CBS Mornings" for a live town hall where they took questions from kids just weeks after returning from their historic moon mission.

item.title: Artemis II crew reveals what snack they ate right after splashdown
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts said they actually really enjoyed the space food, but it was a familiar candy they enjoyed after splashing down in the Pacific Ocean.

item.title: Oil prices hit wartime peak, with Brent crude touching $126 a barrel
item.description: Brent crude surged past $126 a barrel early Thursday, while U.S. gasoline prices jumped to $4.30 a gallon.

item.title: SPLC seeks disclosure of grand jury transcripts in criminal case
item.description: The Southern Poverty Law Center accused senior Justice Department officials of making "misleading" statements after indictment.

item.title: Former federal prosecutors see legal flaws in DOJ's SPLC indictment
item.description: Former federal prosecutors think the indictment struggles to articulate the elements of the alleged crimes in the case, a problem that could lead to its full or partial dismissal.

item.title: DOJ charges Southern Poverty Law Center with fraud
item.description: A federal grand jury indicted the Southern Poverty Law Center on wire and bank fraud-related charges on Tuesday, the Justice Department says, accusing it of paying members of extremist groups as part of its efforts to investigate them.
